skill.name: implementing-command-palettes
skill.description: Guides developers through building reliable Cmd+K command palettes in React, including keyboard navigation, filtering, scrolling, and stable command registration.
skill.summary: Build React command palettes with reliable keyboard navigation, filtering, scrolling, and stable references.
skill.author: AgentWorkforce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown backticks, TypeScript examples, and ordinary UI text. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are caused by inline Markdown code and TypeScript code fences, not Ruby backtick execution. The examples describe React state, effects, refs, and browser event listeners.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown inline code or TypeScript examples in a documentation file. They are not executable skill scripts and contain no shell command invocation path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Key Pattern Matches Keyboard Event Text
low_findings.description: The sensitive file finding at line 90 matches the TypeScript expression e.key inside a keyboard handler. It does not reference certificates, private keys, secrets, or filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 90 is part of a switch statement over KeyboardEvent.key. The semantic context is browser keyboard navigation, not credential or key file handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Patterns in UI Documentation
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to the skill description and shortcut matching examples. No hashing, encryption, random generation, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines discuss Cmd+K behavior and string matching for command shortcuts. There is no cryptographic operation to weaken or misuse.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are ordinary prose about command palettes and React refs. The file contains no host inspection, process listing, environment probing, or system inventory logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documentation about UI behavior and stale closures. No executable reconnaissance command or data collection behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Command palettes often fail because keyboard navigation, focus, filtering, and registration state interact poorly. This skill gives React patterns that keep Cmd+K menus responsive, predictable, and maintainable.
actual_capabilities: Explains wrapper component patterns for mounting command palette effects only when open.
Shows arrow key navigation with selected index clamping and Enter or Escape handling.
Demonstrates scrollIntoView patterns that keep the selected item visible.
Covers command filtering by label, shortcut text, and numeric identifiers.
Describes stable icon, handler, and command registration patterns to avoid render loops.
Provides a testing checklist for command palette keyboard and filtering behavior.
limitations: Focused on React and TypeScript examples, not other frontend frameworks.
Does not provide a complete packaged command palette component.
Does not cover accessibility audits beyond keyboard behavior patterns.
Assumes the application already has routing, command data, and styling decisions.

use_cases.title: Add Cmd+K Navigation to a React App
use_cases.description: Use the patterns to implement open, close, arrow navigation, selection, and command execution behavior.

use_cases.title: Fix Command Palette Render Loops
use_cases.description: Use stable references and label-based registration checks when commands keep re-registering during renders.

use_cases.title: Improve Search and Shortcut Matching
use_cases.description: Match commands by labels, shortcut strings, and item numbers so users can find actions quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Palette
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new command palette.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-command-palettes skill to design a basic React Cmd+K palette with open, close, arrow navigation, and Enter selection.

prompt_templates.title: Add Filtering and Shortcuts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-command-palettes skill to add filtering by command label and shortcut text to my existing React command palette.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Scroll and Focus Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-command-palettes skill to review my palette focus, window key listener, and selected-item scrolling behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Stabilize Command Registration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced render loop and reference stability work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-command-palettes skill to refactor my command registration so icons, handlers, and command identifiers stay stable.

output_examples.input: A React palette moves selection with arrows but scrolls the page too.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies missing preventDefault handling and recommends a window keydown listener with selected index clamping.

output_examples.input: A command list re-registers on every render and causes maximum update depth errors.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains stable icon constants, memoized handlers, and label-based command identifiers to stop unnecessary registration.

output_examples.input: Users type gd but the Go Dashboard shortcut is stored as g d.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends normalizing shortcut text by removing spaces before matching it against the query.

best_practices: Mount command palette effects only while the palette is open.
Reset the selected index whenever the query changes.
Keep command icons, handlers, and registration identifiers stable across renders.
anti_patterns: Do not leave keyboard listeners active after the palette closes.
Do not recreate command objects in ways that trigger repeated registration.
Do not use scrolling behavior that moves visible items unnecessarily.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets React with TypeScript examples and common hook patterns.

faq.question: Does it provide a full command palette package?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and patterns for your own component.

faq.question: Can it help with Cmd+K and Ctrl+K behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on command palette behavior commonly opened with Cmd+K or Ctrl+K.

faq.question: Does it cover keyboard navigation?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers arrow keys, Enter, Escape, focus, and selected index management.

faq.question: Can it fix infinite re-render problems?
faq.answer: It explains stable references and command registration patterns that prevent common render loops.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only React examples and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
